details.body:
finding_summary: The branch makes `## Nearby work` a required `bead_execution_prompt/1` section without a compatibility path for the older `/1` prompts that are already persisted on existing tasks. `BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_CONTRACT_VERSION` is still `1`, but `SECTION_NEARBY_WORK` was added to `BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_SECTION_TITLES` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:18-19,42-53`, and the renderer now always emits that section in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1217-1231`. Older version-1 prompts generated before this bead therefore fail `validate_canonical_prompt_shape`, which disables canonical scope handling in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:519-521` and causes prompt-review / override validation to reject unchanged historical prompts in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:426-435`.
remediation_hint: Either introduce a new contract version and keep consumers able to recognize/validate legacy v1 prompts, or keep `Nearby work` optional when validating older `/1` prompts.

finding_summary: `build_nearby_bead_context()` is treating hierarchy links as nearby dependency work. It passes the full `bead.dependencies` and `bead.dependents` arrays into `nearby_entries_from_relations()` (`src/cli/project.rs:2434-2437`), and that helper never filters by `DependencyKind` (`src/cli/project.rs:2457-2480`). Any bead with a `parent_child` link to its epic will therefore show that epic under `## Nearby work` as a direct dependency, and epics can likewise surface child beads as direct dependents. That misstates the relationship the section is supposed to convey and spends the nearby-context budget on hierarchy metadata instead of actual dependency/sibling context.
affected_files: src/cli/project.rs:2434-2480
remediation_hint: only include `DependencyKind::Blocks` edges in the direct dependency/dependent subsections; keep `parent_child` links for parent/sibling inference only.

finding_summary: the new `## Nearby work` section is not actually preserved by canonical-prompt validation. `is_optional_canonical_section()` marks `Nearby work` optional for every `bead_execution_prompt/1` prompt (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:55-58`), and `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` therefore accepts prompts that omit it (`src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:342-346` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:388-392`). Prompt-review drift detection relies on that validator (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339`), so a rewritten prompt can silently drop the new section and still be accepted as canonical. That makes the feature non-durable in normal workflows: the prompt starts with nearby context, then prompt review can remove it without any rejection.
affected_files: src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:55-58,342-346,388-392; src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339
remediation_hint: either bump the contract version for the new shape or keep legacy compatibility in read-only consumers while requiring `## Nearby work` on newly generated/refined canonical prompts.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:426-435` now require `validate_current_canonical_prompt_shape`, which rejects canonical v1 prompts that legitimately omit `## Nearby work`. That bypasses the compatibility path intentionally added in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:298-306`, so prompt review and `project create-from-bead --prompt-file ...` can no longer accept previously generated canonical task prompts unless the caller invents a Nearby work section it was never given. Keep the stricter validator for freshly generated prompts, but preserve legacy-compatible validation when the source prompt predates this section or version-bump the contract before making the new heading mandatory everywhere.

`src/cli/project.rs:2457-2460` drops every non-`blocks` relation from `Nearby work`, so parent/child edges never appear in `direct_dependencies` or `direct_dependents`. Because bead creation still accepts any open/in-progress bead type (`src/cli/project.rs:1396-1423`), epic beads lose all child-bead scope summaries in `Direct dependents`, and child beads lose their parent epic in `Direct dependencies`, even though the bead spec says these subsections should summarize the focal bead's direct dependency/dependent IDs. If you want to avoid duplicate parent-epic noise for leaf beads, that needs a narrower rule than excluding the entire `parent-child` relation class.

`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:427-430` explicitly accepts contract-marked prompt overrides that omit `## Nearby work`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-340` also treats that legacy shape as valid. But `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:182-190` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:366-367` still inject guidance that says `Nearby work` is an authoritative canonical section whenever the marker is present. On a supported legacy override, prompt-review/completion/final-review agents are told to preserve and use a section that does not exist, which can create false contract-drift or scope complaints.

`src/cli/project.rs:2586-2606` never truncates `NearbyBeadEntry.title` (and even falls back to the title for `scope_summary`), while `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:92-111` can only get back under budget by dropping whole entries and `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:140-148` renders the full title verbatim. A single long bead title can therefore blow the nearby-work byte budget or cause the highest-priority direct dependency to be removed entirely instead of being shortened, violating the per-entry and section-budget requirements.

finding_summary: The new `Nearby work` IDs cannot actually be used for `planned-elsewhere` routing. Review-stage guidance is still built from `extract_pe_bead_ids` only (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:191-195`), the classifier prompt still says `planned-elsewhere` only applies to beads listed under `Already Planned Elsewhere` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:417-425`), and `final_review.rs` strips any `mapped_to_bead_id` that is not in that PE-only allowlist before voting and again before restart routing (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1111-1123`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1844-1858`). When a prompt has Nearby work but no PE entries, every mapping to a sibling/dependent/related bead is discarded, so this bead's core end-to-end behavior still does not work.

finding_summary: Sibling inference and one-line scope summaries currently depend on `br list` returning fields that the load path does not guarantee. `load_bead_summaries` only loads `br list --all --deferred --limit=0 --json` (`src/cli/project.rs:1837-1852`), but `nearby_sibling_entries` reads `summary.dependencies` to recover the parent epic (`src/cli/project.rs:2492-2522`, `src/cli/project.rs:2579-2585`) and nearby entry rendering reads `summary.description` for the scope line (`src/cli/project.rs:2473-2480`, `src/cli/project.rs:2611-2617`). Those fields are optional/defaulted on `BeadSummary` (`src/adapters/br_models.rs:253-267`), so a summary-shaped list response silently yields no siblings and title-only scope summaries, with no fallback.

finding_summary: `Nearby work` can still include closed/deferred siblings when the builder is relying on parent-detail fallback rather than `br list` summaries. In `load_nearby_dependency_details`, sibling prefetching only filters `parent_detail.dependents` by `relation.status` at [src/cli/project.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:1897), and `nearby_sibling_entries` repeats the same summary/relation-only check at [src/cli/project.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:2690). But `nearby_entry_from_relation` later prefers the fetched bead detail status at [src/cli/project.rs](/home/user/ralph-burning/src/cli/project.rs:2619). If the parent relation omits status and the sibling summary is missing/sparse, a closed sibling loaded via `br show` is still admitted, rendered as closed in `## Nearby work`, and can even consume one of the 5 sibling slots ahead of an open sibling. That violates the requirement to omit closed/deferred nearby beads entirely and can mislead planned-elsewhere routing.
remediation_hint: Filter sibling candidates using detail status when it is available, both before the prefetch `take(NEARBY_SIBLING_LIMIT)` and before inserting the rendered sibling entry, and add a regression test where only the fetched sibling detail carries `status=closed`.

finding_summary: `Nearby work` can still surface closed/deferred beads under `Related work` when `br list` is stale. In `src/cli/project.rs:2755-2766`, related-work selection only filters on `BeadSummary.status`, even though `src/cli/project.rs:1956-1964` prefetches `br show` details for those same candidates. If the summary says a related bead is open but the detail says `closed` or `deferred`, the prompt still renders it (now with the closed status) and downstream review/final_review routing will treat that bead as a valid planned-elsewhere target.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:1956-1964
- src/cli/project.rs:2755-2827
remediation_hint: Filter related-work candidates with `nearby_candidate_status_is_omitted(...)` using `bead_details` before applying the top-3 limit, and add a regression test for summary-open/detail-closed related beads.

finding_summary: Prompt-review drift validation does not preserve the legacy prompt shape. In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-340`, when the original prompt is a legacy v1 prompt without `## Nearby work`, the refined prompt is checked with the compatibility validator, so a refiner can add a brand-new `## Nearby work` section and still pass validation. That section is supposed to be graph-derived builder output, not free-form prompt-review text; once accepted, any fabricated nearby bead IDs become valid planned-elsewhere routing targets in later review stages.
severity: low
affected_files:
- src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:333-340
remediation_hint: When the original prompt is legacy-without-nearby, require the refined prompt to stay on that same section shape unless there is an explicit migration path that rebuilds nearby-work content from bead data.

src/cli/project.rs:1912-1928 and 2788-2800 cap summary-inferred sibling detail prefetch at five IDs before detail filtering. When the parent epic detail cannot be loaded and one of those first five summaries is stale-open but actually closed, that sibling is dropped without promoting the sixth open sibling, so the `Nearby work` section can under-report available siblings and hide legitimate planned-elsewhere ownership.

src/cli/project.rs:1863-1966 and 2773-2785 now eagerly and sequentially run `br show` for every dependency/dependent, the parent epic, up to 20 sibling candidates, and a bounded related-work set before rendering the prompt. On moderately connected beads this turns task creation into dozens of subprocesses, making prompt creation latency scale with graph degree and label density.
